name: Horror Movies
description: Classic scares, iconic villains, and modern nightmares

questions.q: Who directs the 1960 horror classic 'Psycho,' featuring the infamous shower scene?

source.notes: Based on Robert Bloch's novel. Set at the Bates Motel.

questions.q: Who plays the telekinetic title character in the 1976 film 'Carrie'?

source.notes: Based on Stephen King's debut novel. The pig's blood scene became iconic.

questions.q: In 'The Shining,' what is the name of the haunted hotel?

source.notes: Exterior was filmed at Timberline Lodge in Oregon. King based it on the Stanley Hotel in Colorado.

questions.q: What is the name of the demonic doll in the 'Child's Play' franchise?

source.notes: Possessed by serial killer Charles Lee Ray. Voiced by Brad Dourif.

questions.q: Who wrote the 1897 novel 'Dracula'?

source.notes: Epistolary novel. Title character inspired by Vlad III Dracula (Vlad the Impaler).

questions.q: What is the name of the burned, claw-fingered villain who haunts teens' dreams on Elm Street?

source.notes: Played by Robert Englund. Created by Wes Craven. Red-and-green striped sweater.

questions.q: Which actor played Count Dracula in the classic 1931 Universal horror film?

source.notes: Hungarian-American actor. Became typecast as horror villains for life.

questions.q: Which 1973 film features a young girl named Regan possessed by a demon?

source.notes: Directed by William Friedkin. Based on William Peter Blatty's novel.

questions.q: In 'The Omen,' what is the name of the demonic child?

source.notes: Damien Thorn. He bears the 666 birthmark identifying him as the Antichrist.

questions.q: Which 2017 horror film written and directed by Jordan Peele won him an Oscar for Best Original Screenplay?

source.notes: 'Get Out' starred Daniel Kaluuya. Nominated for Best Picture.

questions.q: Who made his directorial debut with the critically acclaimed 2018 horror film 'Hereditary'?

source.notes: Starred Toni Collette. Aster followed with 'Midsommar' (2019).

questions.q: What is the name of Jordan Peele's 2019 horror film about families meeting their own doppelgängers?

source.notes: Starred Lupita Nyong'o in a dual role. Second film written and directed by Peele.

questions.q: 'The Conjuring' films are based on the paranormal investigations of which real-life couple?

source.notes: Ed and Lorraine Warren. Their cases also inspired 'The Amityville Horror.'

questions.q: In 'Invasion of the Body Snatchers,' what replaces humans with perfect duplicates?

source.notes: Grown in large seed pods. First film released 1956.

questions.q: Which 1974 film introduced the chainsaw-wielding killer Leatherface?

source.notes: Directed by Tobe Hooper. Loosely inspired by Ed Gein.

questions.q: What is the name of the cursed tape that kills viewers in 'The Ring' (2002)?

source.notes: After watching, a phone rings and says 'Seven days.' American remake of Japanese 'Ringu.'

questions.q: Which 1960 film is often credited as the first 'slasher'-genre film?

source.notes: Hitchcock. 'Peeping Tom' (also 1960) shares credit.

questions.q: Who is the masked killer in the 'Friday the 13th' franchise (from Part 2 onward)?

source.notes: His mother Pamela is the killer in the first film. Jason wears his iconic hockey mask starting in Part 3.

questions.q: In 'Scream' (1996), what is the killer's iconic mask known as?

source.notes: Inspired by Edvard Munch's painting 'The Scream.' Mass-produced Halloween mask.

questions.q: The 1999 found-footage indie 'The Blair Witch Project' is set in what U.S. state?

source.notes: Burkittsville, Maryland. Made for ~$60k, grossed $249M.
